8.reason: [Error: Cannot initialize struct members in C; use assignment in main or a constructor function]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Cannot initialize struct members in C; use assignment in main or a constructor function]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Cannot initialize members inside struct definition in C; use assignment in main or a constructor function]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Initializers are not allowed in a struct definition in C (syntax error)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Invalid: cannot initialize struct members in C; use assignment after declaration.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  In C, struct members cannot be initialized with '=' inside the struct definition. This causes a compilation error. They should be declared without initialization (e.g., 'int x0;').
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  In C, struct members cannot be initialized with '=' inside the struct definition. This causes a compilation error. They should be declared as 'int x0;' and initialized later or using designated initializers in C99+.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [C does not allow initialization of struct members at declaration; this syntax is invalid in C (allowed only in C++).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [C does not allow initialization of struct members at declaration; this syntax is invalid in C (valid only in C++).]

33.reason: [Error: Assignment '=' used instead of comparison '=='; should be p[j].y0 == p[i].y1]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Assignment '=' used instead of comparison '=='; should be p[j].y0 == p[i].y1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Use of assignment '=' instead of equality '==' in condition; should be p[j].y0 == p[i].y1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Should be '==' instead of '=' for equality check (logical error)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Logical error: assignment (=) used instead of equality (==); should be p[j].y0==p[i].y1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  The condition uses assignment '=' instead of comparison '==' for y-coordinates (p[j].y0=p[i].y1). This assigns the value and evaluates to true if non-zero, leading to incorrect logic. It should be 'p[j].y0==p[i].y1'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  The second part of the condition uses assignment '=' instead of comparison '=='. It should be 'p[j].y0 == p[i].y1'. This causes logical errors and potentially incorrect returns.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Assignment operator '=' used instead of equality operator '==' for y-coordinate comparison, causing logical error and unintended assignment.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Assignment operator '=' used instead of equality operator '==' for y-coordinate comparison, causing logical error and unintended assignment.]
